description:
You are a network engineer doing a quick post-mortem on a WAN outage.  
A raw probe log is already present on the workstation:

    /home/user/logs/ping_results.log

Each line is a single record using the exact five-field, pipe-delimited layout shown below.

    TIMESTAMP|SRC_HOST|DST_IP|LATENCY_MS|STATUS

Example (do **not** rewrite the file, it is already on disk):

    2023-04-01T12:00:01Z|router1|8.8.8.8|24|reachable

Goal  
1. Produce a latency-only CSV file by cutting columns and changing the delimiter.  
   • Path: /home/user/output/latency_report.csv  
   • Keep the original line order.  
   • Include exactly the SRC_HOST, DST_IP and LATENCY_MS fields (columns 2,3,4).  
   • Use a single comma “,” as the separator with **no leading or trailing spaces**.  
   • The file must contain one record per input line; eight lines are expected.  
   • Blank latency values are represented by a literal hyphen character “-”.  
   • Example target line format: router1,8.8.8.8,24

2. Extract every probe that failed (STATUS field value “unreachable”) into a second
   log that the NOC can import.  
   • Path: /home/user/output/unreachable.log  
   • Keep only TIMESTAMP and the word “unreachable” separated by one single space.  
   • Preserve the original chronological order.  
   • Exactly two lines are expected.  
   • Example target line format: 2023-04-01T12:15:01Z unreachable

3. Do all of the above entirely with classic text utilities typically available on any
   Linux host (e.g. cut, tr, paste, grep, awk, sort, uniq, etc.).  A sensible solution
   should take roughly 5–10 shell commands in total.

Verification  
Automated tests will check:

• /home/user/output/latency_report.csv exists, is world-readable (0644 or more),
  and contains exactly the eight comma-separated lines described above.  
• /home/user/output/unreachable.log exists, is world-readable (0644 or more),
  and contains exactly the two space-separated lines described above.  
• No extra files are created inside /home/user/output.  

Create the two files in /home/user/output using only the given raw log as input.
truth:
Initial files/directories present before agent starts
-----------------------------------------------------
1. Directory: /home/user/logs         (rwxr-xr-x)
2. Directory: /home/user/output       (rwxrwxr-x)   — empty
3. File: /home/user/logs/ping_results.log (rw-r--r--)
   Exact content (8 lines, Unix LF line endings)

2023-04-01T12:00:01Z|router1|8.8.8.8|24|reachable
2023-04-01T12:05:01Z|router1|1.1.1.1|25|reachable
2023-04-01T12:10:01Z|router1|8.8.4.4|27|reachable
2023-04-01T12:15:01Z|router1|208.67.222.222|-|unreachable
2023-04-01T12:20:01Z|router1|8.8.8.8|26|reachable
2023-04-01T12:25:01Z|router1|1.1.1.1|-|unreachable
2023-04-01T12:30:01Z|router1|8.8.4.4|29|reachable
2023-04-01T12:35:01Z|router1|208.67.222.222|28|reachable


Expected files after successful completion
------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/output/latency_report.csv (rw-r--r--)
   Exact content (8 lines, Unix LF):

router1,8.8.8.8,24
router1,1.1.1.1,25
router1,8.8.4.4,27
router1,208.67.222.222,-
router1,8.8.8.8,26
router1,1.1.1.1,-
router1,8.8.4.4,29
router1,208.67.222.222,28

2. /home/user/output/unreachable.log (rw-r--r--)
   Exact content (2 lines, Unix LF):

2023-04-01T12:15:01Z unreachable
2023-04-01T12:25:01Z unreachable
name: task_000000_93a4ce97
